name: IELTS Daily v2 — Bug Fixes & Polish
description: Fix submission bugs, improve student dashboard layout, redesign admin panel, add ASCII topic art

userStories.title: Fix exercise submission 500 errors
userStories.description: As a student, I want exercise submissions to work so I can get feedback on my answers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add proper error logging with try/catch in POST /s/:token/exercise/:exerciseId — log the full error stack trace to stdout so fly logs captures it

Fix grading service: the error is 'answers.answers.find is not a function' in src/services/grading.ts — the client-side JS is sending a different JSON shape than what the grader expects. Inspect each exercise template's submit JS to see what shape it POSTs, then fix grading.ts to match OR fix the templates to send the expected shape
For long_reading and short_reading: grader expects {answers: [{number, answer}]} — verify client sends this exact shape
For vocabulary: grader expects {matches: [{word, matched_definition}]} — verify client sends this
For fill_gap: grader expects {fills: [{number, word}]} — verify client sends this
For writing_micro: grader expects {text: string} — verify client sends this
All 5 exercise types submit successfully without 500 errors
After submission, the page shows feedback (correct/incorrect for each question, score) — the user should NOT see the submit button again
npm run build passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL BUG. Fly logs show: TypeError: answers.answers.find is not a function and TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'find'). The mismatch is between what the client-side JS in templates POSTs and what src/services/grading.ts expects. Check BOTH sides.

userStories.title: Fix multiple choice letter duplication
userStories.description: As a student, I want clean question display without duplicated option letters.
acceptanceCriteria: Multiple choice options show letter prefix ONLY from the UI (e.g., the radio pill shows 'A)'), not from the answer text itself
If the AI-generated content includes 'A) ...' in the option text, strip the leading letter+paren from the display text
Questions display cleanly as: [A) pill] The actual answer text here
This applies to both long_reading and short_reading templates
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The AI generates options like 'A) Volcanic activity' and the template ALSO adds an 'A)' prefix, resulting in 'A) A) Volcanic activity'. Strip the prefix from content if present.

userStories.title: Add drop cap to article passages
userStories.description: As a student, I want the first letter of reading passages to be large and decorative, like a real newspaper.
acceptanceCriteria: Long reading passage first letter uses CSS drop-cap: float left, large serif font (Playfair Display), ~3 lines tall
Short reading passage also has drop cap
Drop cap works correctly with dark mode
Use CSS initial-letter or float-based approach for browser compatibility
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Classic newspaper typography. Use CSS float:left on a ::first-letter pseudo-element or wrap the first letter in a span.

userStories.title: Redesign student dashboard as masonry newspaper layout
userStories.description: As a student, I want the daily board to look like a newspaper front page with cards of different sizes arranged in a masonry grid.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard uses CSS grid masonry-style layout (use grid-template-rows with different spans, or CSS columns)
Exercise cards have different sizes: long reading gets the largest card (spans 2 rows), short reading medium, vocabulary/fill-gap/writing smaller cards
Each card shows: exercise type label (kicker), title or excerpt, status badge (Disponible or score)
Cards are clickable and link to the exercise page
Layout is responsive: on mobile (< 600px), single column stack; on desktop, 2-3 column masonry
Overall feel: like a real newspaper front page with varied article sizes, not a uniform list
Keep the masthead (The IELTS Daily + date) at the top
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Think NYT or Guardian front page layout. Use CSS grid with explicit row spans for different card sizes. The long reading is the 'lead story' taking up the most space.

userStories.title: Add ASCII topic illustration to dashboard
userStories.description: As a student, I want to see a visual illustration related to today's topic on the dashboard, rendered as ASCII or dithered art.
acceptanceCriteria: One of the masonry cards on the dashboard displays an ASCII art illustration related to today's topic
The ASCII art is generated as part of daily board generation: add an 'illustration' field to the board content
In src/services/content.ts generateBoard(), add a Claude API call that generates ASCII art (30-40 chars wide, 15-20 lines tall) for the topic using block characters and simple shapes
The ASCII art is stored in the boards table (add an 'illustration' TEXT column)
Display in a <pre> tag with monospace font, styled to fit the masonry card
Art should be simple, recognizable silhouettes — think: dinosaur outline, coral reef shapes, mountain range, etc.
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The user wants visual interest on the dashboard. ASCII art generated by Claude for each topic. Keep it simple — silhouettes and outlines, not photorealistic. Store in DB alongside the board.

userStories.title: Compact activity heatmap
userStories.description: As a student, I want the streak and activity section to take less vertical space on the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Reduce heatmap from 16 weeks to 8 weeks (56 days)
Use smaller cells: 8px x 8px with 2px gap instead of current 14px
Heatmap renders horizontally compact — should take no more than ~60px height total
Streak number and heatmap sit side by side on desktop (not stacked)
On mobile: streak above, heatmap below, both compact
Remove day-of-week labels (Mon, Wed, Fri) to save space — just show the colored grid
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The current heatmap dominates the dashboard. Make it a subtle accent, not the hero. Think of it as a small badge/ribbon, not a full section.

userStories.title: Admin dashboard — sidebar layout with shadcn-style design
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a proper dashboard layout with sidebar navigation, not a single scrolling page.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin dashboard uses a sidebar + main content layout (CSS grid: 240px sidebar, 1fr content)
Sidebar contains navigation links: Today's Board, Users, Topics, Email Log, Settings
Each nav item shows as a section in the main content area (use anchor links or show/hide sections with JS)
Sidebar is fixed/sticky on scroll
Design: clean, modern — Inter font throughout, subtle gray sidebar background (#F8F8F8), white content area, proper spacing
Topbar shows: app name, current date, admin user indicator
Settings is NOT always visible — it's its own section accessed from the sidebar, not appended at the bottom
Mobile: sidebar collapses to a hamburger menu
Overall aesthetic matches modern dashboards like Linear, Vercel, or shadcn examples — not a 90s admin panel
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is server-rendered HTML (Hono templates), not React. Build the layout with CSS grid and vanilla JS for section toggling. The user referenced shadcn — match that clean, minimal aesthetic with Inter font and subtle borders.

userStories.title: Admin — improved tables with actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I want proper data tables with sorting, row actions, and clean styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Users table: columns for Name, Email, Streak, Last Active, Today (X/5), Total, Actions dropdown (View Dashboard, Remove)
Email log table: columns for Date, Topic, Recipients, Status badge (green/red), Duration, Actions (Resend for failed)
Topic queue table: columns for Position, Topic, Last Used, Times Used, Actions (Force Next, Remove)
Tables have: zebra striping (subtle alternating row backgrounds), hover highlight, proper padding
Action buttons use dropdown menus (click to expand) not inline links
Empty states: show 'No users yet' / 'No emails sent' with subtle message instead of empty table
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Style tables to look like shadcn/Tanstack table — clean borders, good spacing, action dropdowns. All vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, no React.

userStories.title: Server error logging and health endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want proper error logging so I can debug production issues from fly logs.
acceptanceCriteria: All route handlers wrapped in try/catch that logs: timestamp, method, path, error message, stack trace
Use Hono's onError handler to catch unhandled errors globally
Log format: JSON lines — {timestamp, method, path, error, stack} so logs are grep-able
Add GET /health endpoint that returns 200 with {status: ok, timestamp, boardExists: bool}
Add request logging middleware: log method, path, status code, duration for every request
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is critical infrastructure. Without proper logs, debugging production is blind. Fly.io captures stdout/stderr.
